description: Figure 3 presents small-angle X-ray scattering (SAXS) profiles showing the logarithmic scattering intensity as a function of the scattering vector q (nm⁻¹) for Fe-Al based low-density steels with varying aluminum contents: Fe-5AL (circles), Fe-6AL (squares), Fe-6.8AL (triangles), and Fe-9AL (diamonds). The open symbols represent experimental data, while the solid blue lines indicate fitted curves using a local monodisperse model with log-normal distribution (Eq. 3). The curves are vertically offset for clarity. The broken dotted lines proportional to q⁻⁴ indicate Porod scattering behavior from sharp interfaces. With increasing Al content from 5 to 9 wt%, the SAXS intensity systematically increases and the Guinier region shifts to lower q values, indicating larger nano-sized ordered Fe₃Al (D₀₃) precipitates. The fitted results reveal particle diameters increasing from 2.5±0.15 nm (Fe-5AL) to 3.75±0.2 nm (Fe-9AL), with corresponding increases in volume fraction. This quantitative nanostructural evidence supports the strengthening mechanism analysis by confirming the evolution of ordered phase characteristics with composition.
